2606.27841.title: WattLayer: Get Layers Right to Estimate Inference Energy of Neural Networks
2606.27841.paper_summary: The widespread adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI) has led to increasing concerns about energy consumption, yet there is a lack of standardized methodologies to accurately estimate AI inference energy consumption, particularly across various tasks and architectures. In this study, we propose a task independent, layer-wise energy estimation model for AI architectures. Our model is evaluated on a large dataset of more than 100,000 layers for 295 neural network architectures across 3 widely-used tasks and 3 distinct hardware platforms. Our approach achieves a median error of 19.6%, outperforming state-of-the-art methods. We further show that layer-wise decomposition generalize to new tasks without complete retraining, by leveraging shared layers across architectures. It offer tools, insights and a precise methodology to empower stakeholders in designing energy-efficient AI systems.
2606.27841.paper_summary_zh: 人工智慧（AI）的廣泛採用引發了對能源消耗日益增長的關注，然而，缺乏標準化的方法來準確估算AI推理的能源消耗，特別是在各種任務和架構之間。在本研究中，我們提出了一種任務無關的、逐層的能源估算模型，用於AI架構。我們的模型在一個包含超過100,000層的龐大數據集上進行評估，涵蓋295種神經網絡架構，涉及3個廣泛使用的任務和3個不同的硬體平台。我們的方法實現了19.6%的中位數誤差，超越了最先進的方法。我們進一步展示了逐層分解能夠在不完全重新訓練的情況下，通用於新任務，通過利用架構之間共享的層。這提供了工具、見解和精確的方法論，以幫助利益相關者設計節能的AI系統。
2606.27841.author: Adrien Sardi et.al.
2606.27841.authors: Adrien Sardi, Marie-Line Alberi Morel, Sara Alouf, Frédéric Giroire, Joanna Moulierac
